message:
perf(mvcc,vdbe,e2e): GC-horizon index, MVCC write profiling, version chain retention fix, cached snapshot safety hardening

Five areas of improvement targeting MVCC commit throughput, observability,
correctness, and hot-path memory allocation.

MVCC GC-horizon tracking (begin_concurrent.rs):

- ConcurrentRegistry now maintains a BTreeMap<CommitSeq, usize> counting
  active session snapshot highs, enabling O(log n) GC-horizon queries
  without locking and scanning every handle. Populated on register,
  cleaned on remove_and_recycle.

- New held_lock_pages() for querying the exact set of page locks tracked
  by a concurrent handle. tracked_write_conflict_pages_iter() avoids
  rebuilding a sorted copy when the caller only needs iteration.

Lock table hardening (core_types.rs):

- holder() now uses the fast-path flat atomic array for pages 1..=65536
  (matching try_acquire/release), avoiding unnecessary shard lookup.

- has_draining AtomicBool guard applied to holder() too — previously
  only try_acquire checked it.

- Rolling rebuild now cleans orphaned entries in the fast_locks array
  BEFORE rotating shard tables, ensuring stale locks from crashed
  transactions are cleared regardless of path.

Version chain retention fix (lifecycle.rs):

- Removed premature version retirement during publish_write_set. The
  prior code called defer_retire_version on the superseded version
  immediately at commit time, which could make it invisible to
  concurrent readers holding older snapshots. Versions now remain in
  the chain until explicit GC.

- Arc::try_unwrap on write_set_data avoids cloning 4KB PageData per
  page when refcount=1 (common case — no savepoints hold a snapshot).
  Falls back to a single HashMap clone when refcount>1.

- SmallVec<[PageNumber; 8]> for FCW conflict collection avoids heap
  allocation for typical transactions with ≤8 conflicts.

- Updated test to verify version chain visibility: superseded versions
  must remain resolvable by earlier snapshots until GC.

Cached read snapshot safety (connection.rs):

- Restricted cached read snapshot reuse to :memory: databases only.
  File-backed databases may have external writers that commit between
  cached reads, making the snapshot stale.

- Added rollback on connection close and rollback of stale re-entrant
  snapshots (possible when connection-fallback subqueries park their
  own read snapshot while the outer VDBE executes).

- Clarified recursive_triggers comment: our table-name check is more
  conservative than SQLite's per-trigger-name check.

VDBE MVCC write profiling (engine.rs):

- New metric helpers: duration_ns_saturating, add_vdbe_counter,
  add_vdbe_counter_if, add_vdbe_duration_if — all gated behind
  FSQLITE_VDBE_METRICS_ENABLED to avoid overhead when profiling is off.

- New counters: tier0_already_owned_writes, tier1_first_touch_writes,
  tier2_commit_surface_writes, page_lock_waits, page_lock_wait_time_ns,
  write_busy_retries/timeouts, stale_snapshot_rejects,
  page_one_conflict_tracks, pending_commit_surface_clears (count + time),
  page_data_normalization metrics.

Hot-path profiling infrastructure (perf_runner.rs + report.rs + e2e):

- New HotPathMvccWriteProfile struct capturing all MVCC write tier
  counters, lock wait metrics, and retry breakdown.

- New HotPathPageDataMotionProfile struct for borrowed/owned write
  normalization, passthrough, resize, and zero-fill byte accounting.

- HotPathRetryBreakdown in report.rs for structured retry diagnostics.

- page_data_normalization_bytes_total added to HotPathAllocatorPressure.

- realdb_e2e and fsqlite_executor updated to populate the new profile
  sections.

Also updates beads issue tracker metadata.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

+}
+
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]

+}

+        normalized_payload_bytes_total: snapshot

+            .page_data_motion
+            .normalized_payload_bytes_total,

     let parser_time_ns = parser
         .parse_time_ns

@@ -928,6 +1087,114 @@ pub fn render_hot_path_profile_markdown(report: &HotPathProfileReport) -> String
     }
     let _ = writeln!(out);
 
+    let _ = writeln!(out, "## MVCC Write Path\n");
+    let _ = writeln!(
+        out,
+        "- Writes total: {} (tier0={}, tier1={}, tier2={})",
+        report.mvcc_write.total_write_attempts,
+        report.mvcc_write.tier0_already_owned_writes_total,
+        report.mvcc_write.tier1_first_touch_writes_total,
+        report.mvcc_write.tier2_commit_surface_writes_total
+    );
+    let _ = writeln!(
+        out,
+        "- Page-touch classes: already_owned={} first_touch={} commit_surface={} page_one_tracks={} pending_surface_clears={}",
+        report.mvcc_write.tier0_already_owned_writes_total,
+        report.mvcc_write.tier1_first_touch_writes_total,
+        report.mvcc_write.tier2_commit_surface_writes_total,
+        report.mvcc_write.page_one_conflict_tracks_total,
+        report.mvcc_write.pending_commit_surface_clears_total
+    );
+    let _ = writeln!(
+        out,
+        "- Page-lock waits: {} (time_ns={})",
+        report.mvcc_write.page_lock_waits_total, report.mvcc_write.page_lock_wait_time_ns_total
+    );
+    let _ = writeln!(
+        out,
+        "- BUSY retries/timeouts: {}/{}",
+        report.mvcc_write.write_busy_retries_total, report.mvcc_write.write_busy_timeouts_total
+    );
+    let _ = writeln!(
+        out,
+        "- Runtime retry taxonomy: total={} aborts={} kind[busy={},busy_snapshot={},busy_recovery={},other={}] phase[begin={},body={},commit={},rollback={}] max_batch_attempts={}",
+        report.mvcc_write.runtime_retry.total_retries,
+        report.mvcc_write.runtime_retry.total_aborts,
+        report.mvcc_write.runtime_retry.kind.busy,
+        report.mvcc_write.runtime_retry.kind.busy_snapshot,
+        report.mvcc_write.runtime_retry.kind.busy_recovery,
+        report.mvcc_write.runtime_retry.kind.busy_other,
+        report.mvcc_write.runtime_retry.phase.begin,
+        report.mvcc_write.runtime_retry.phase.body,
+        report.mvcc_write.runtime_retry.phase.commit,
+        report.mvcc_write.runtime_retry.phase.rollback,
+        report.mvcc_write.runtime_retry.max_batch_attempts
+    );
+    if !report
+        .mvcc_write
+        .runtime_retry
+        .top_snapshot_conflict_pages
+        .is_empty()
+    {
+        let top_pages = report
+            .mvcc_write
+            .runtime_retry
+            .top_snapshot_conflict_pages
+            .iter()
+            .map(|entry| format!("p{}:{}", entry.page_no, entry.retries))
+            .collect::<Vec<_>>()
+            .join(", ");
+        let _ = writeln!(out, "- Top snapshot conflict pages: {top_pages}");
+    }
+    if let Some(last_busy_message) = &report.mvcc_write.runtime_retry.last_busy_message {
+        let _ = writeln!(out, "- Last retry message: `{last_busy_message}`");
+    }
+    let _ = writeln!(
+        out,
+        "- Stale snapshot rejects: {}",
+        report.mvcc_write.stale_snapshot_rejects_total
+    );
+    let _ = writeln!(
+        out,
+        "- Page-one conflict tracking: {} (time_ns={})",
+        report.mvcc_write.page_one_conflict_tracks_total,
+        report.mvcc_write.page_one_conflict_track_time_ns_total
+    );
+    let _ = writeln!(
+        out,
+        "- Pending surface clears: {} (time_ns={})",
+        report.mvcc_write.pending_commit_surface_clears_total,
+        report.mvcc_write.pending_commit_surface_clear_time_ns_total
+    );
+    let _ = writeln!(out);
+
+    let _ = writeln!(out, "## PageData Motion\n");
+    let _ = writeln!(
+        out,
+        "- Borrowed normalization calls: {} (exact-size copies={})",
+        report
+            .page_data_motion
+            .borrowed_write_normalization_calls_total,
+        report.page_data_motion.borrowed_exact_size_copies_total
+    );
+    let _ = writeln!(
+        out,
+        "- Owned normalization calls: {} (passthrough={}, resized_copies={})",
+        report
+            .page_data_motion
+            .owned_write_normalization_calls_total,
+        report.page_data_motion.owned_passthrough_total,
+        report.page_data_motion.owned_resized_copies_total
+    );
+    let _ = writeln!(
+        out,
+        "- Normalized bytes: {} (payload={}, zero_fill={})",
+        report.page_data_motion.normalized_bytes_total,
+        report.page_data_motion.normalized_payload_bytes_total,
+        report.page_data_motion.normalized_zero_fill_bytes_total
+    );
+    let _ = writeln!(out);
+
     let _ = writeln!(out, "## Ranked Hotspots\n");
     for entry in &actionable_ranking.named_hotspots {
         let _ = writeln!(
@@ -1091,7 +1358,7 @@ pub fn render_hot_path_profile_markdown(report: &HotPathProfileReport) -> String
     );
     let _ = writeln!(
         out,
-        "- `actionable_ranking.json` — hotspot, reuse, and baseline-waste ledger for follow-on Track I/Track J work"
+        "- `actionable_ranking.json` — hotspot, MVCC, reuse, and baseline-waste ledger for follow-on Track E/Track J work"
     );
     let _ = writeln!(
         out,
@@ -1114,6 +1381,18 @@ fn hotspot_implication(subsystem: &str) -> (&'static str, &'static [&'static str
             "J2/J6/J7 target: result-row materialization is still paying avoidable clone/allocation and reusable-frame cost in the mixed hot path.",
             &["bd-db300.10.2", "bd-db300.10.6", "bd-db300.10.7"],
         ),
+        "mvcc_page_lock_wait" => (
+            "E2.1/E5.1 target: measured page-lock wait time is a first-class MVCC tax, so publish shrink and disjoint-page topology should move before deeper executor tuning.",
+            &["bd-db300.5.2.1", "bd-db300.5.5.1"],
+        ),
+        "mvcc_page_one_conflict_tracking" => (
+            "E5.1/E5.2 target: page-one conflict-only tracking is visible enough to justify home-lane/extent runway work that keeps disjoint inserts physically disjoint longer.",
+            &["bd-db300.5.5.1", "bd-db300.5.5.2"],
+        ),
+        "mvcc_pending_commit_surface_clear" => (
+            "E2.1/E2.2 target: synthetic pending-surface cleanup is now measured directly, so the next metadata-plane cut should shrink or bypass this publish-side maintenance.",
